title: Cascade of magnetic-field-induced quantum spin states in a spin-1 honeycomb magnet
authors: Kaixin Tang, Zhao-Yang Dong, Yanyan Shangguan, Zhao Gong, Ye Yang, Song Bao, Houpu Li, Nan Zhang, Hongyu Li, Jian-Xin Li, Jinsheng Wen, Ziji Xiang, Xianhui Chen
abstract: Quantum fluctuations endow spin systems with surprisingly enriched magnetic phase diagrams. In frustrated magnets, strong quantum fluctuations boosted by either geometrical incompatibility or competitive exchange interactions stabilize cascades of unusual phases of matter. Here we reveal the presence of multiple quantum phases in the honeycomb antiferromagnet Na$_{3}$Ni$_{2}$BiO$_{6}$, both inside and beyond its field-induced one-third magnetization plateau. Comprehensive measurements of thermodynamic quantities demonstrate that the one-third plateau comprises at least three distinct spin states with nearly-degenerate net magnetization, separated by first-order transitions that likely involve sequential spin reconfiguration. Upon further increasing the magnetic field, the system evolves across a myriad of peculiar phases before reaching full polarization; these intermediate phases possess copious low-energy excitations, manifested by anomalous upturns of specific heat at ultralow temperatures -- probably hinting at the development of "hidden" ordered ground states. The complex magnetic phase diagram of Na$_{3}$Ni$_{2}$BiO$_{6}$ underlines the preponderant impact of quantum fluctuations on a honeycomb spin lattice with strong exchange frustration.
summary: This paper demonstrates that the 1/3-magnetization plateau in the honeycomb magnet Na3Ni2BiO6 is not a single phase but a complex sequence of multiple quantum spin states. The discovery of a cascade of field-induced phases and anomalous low-temperature excitations highlights the profound impact of quantum fluctuations in frustrated lattices.
main_problem: The study aims to understand the complex magnetic phase diagram and the nature of field-induced quantum spin states in a highly frustrated honeycomb antiferromagnet, specifically investigating the substructure of the 1/3-magnetization plateau.
main_result: The researchers revealed that the 1/3-magnetization plateau in Na3Ni2BiO6 is composed of at least three distinct spin states and that a cascade of additional quantum phases exists between the plateau and magnetic saturation, accompanied by anomalous low-temperature specific heat upturns.
method: The study employs a variety of experimental probes including AC magnetostriction via a magnetoelectric composite laminate, DC magnetization, magnetic torque magnetometry, and specific heat measurements down to 0.1 K, supplemented by DFT calculations.
model_or_system: The physical system is Na3Ni2BiO6, a spin-1 (S=1) quasi-2D honeycomb antiferromagnet characterized by strong exchange frustration and potential Kitaev-like interactions.
key_observables: Magnetization (M), magnetostriction coefficient (alpha_x), magnetic torque (tau), specific heat (Cp), magnetic Grünevensen parameter (Gamma_H), and magnetic entropy (S).
important_parameters: Magnetic fields up to 14 T, temperatures down to 0.1 K, Neel temperature (TN approx 10 K), and critical fields (Hc1 through Hc5).
main_assumptions: The phase diagram is assumed to be largely controlled by zero-temperature quantum fluctuations rather than thermal or entropic effects.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the experimental setup and magnetization/magnetostriction data; Figure 2 compares anomalies in different probes; Figure 3 displays specific heat evolution and power-law scaling; Figure 4 presents the comprehensive H-T phase diagram.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the material and problem, describes the experimental setup and magnetoelectric technique, presents magnetization and magnetostriction results revealing the plateau substructure, analyzes specific heat anomalies and scaling, constructs the H-T phase diagram, and concludes with theoretical implications.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Fig. 1. Magnetic-field-induced phase transitions and H −T phase diagram for Na3Ni2BiO6. a, A schematic setup of the Na3Ni2BiO6/PMN-PT composite laminate used in the ac magnetostriction measurement (Methods). b, The real part of the magnetoelectric (ME) coefficient, αx(H) ∝λab(H) (see Methods), measured in Na3Ni2BiO6 with H ∥c∗. Orange shaded area represents the 1/3 magnetization plateau. Field induced phase transitions are marked by dashed and dash-dotted lines. Red diamonds and dark yellow down triangles indicate the unla- belled phase transitions Hc3 and Hc4, respectively. “∗” mark the crossover to a single-domain state. Data are plotted in the square-root coordinate and offset vertically...

Fig. 2. Low-temperature thermodynamic investigations of the 1/3-plateau phases. a, Comparison of the positions of anomalies in αx(H) (upper), d(τ/H)/dH (middle) and dM/dH (bottom) measured at T = 1.5-2.0 K. Solid (dashed) lines represent data collected during the field up-sweeps (down-sweeps). The vertical thin bars indicate the anomalies at transition fields H∗ 1 and H∗ 2. b, M(H) measured at 0.4 K up to µ0H = 7 T. The horizontal black bar denotes the value of Ms/3. Red, olive and blue symbols are data points for the plateau phases I, II and III, respectively, extracted from M(T) measurements under fixed H (see panel d). Arrows indicate the direction of field sweeps. c, Magnetic torque...

Fig. 3. Divergent low-T specific heat and its field evolution. a, Specific heat divided by tem- perature, Cp/T, plotted against T from 0.1 to 4 K under varying H ∥c∗. Data are offset vertically for clarity. b, An expanded view of Cp(T)/T below 0.4 K for 7.3 T ≤µ0H ≤11.5 T. Inset shows the H dependence of Cp measured at different T, different background colours distinguish magnetic states: zigzag AFM (yellow), 1/3-Ms plateau (cyan), intermediate phases (light grey) and polarized state (magenta). c, The H-dependent change of magnetic entropy, ∆S = S(H, T) −S(H, 0.1 K), calculated by integrating Cp/T with respect to T from 0.1 K to the referred temperatures. Data are normalized to R ln3, where...

Fig. 4. Low-temperature H −T phase diagram determined from Cp and τ(H). a, Phase diagram of Na3Ni2BiO6 for H ∥c∗, overlaid onto the contour plots of Cp/T. Open grey squares, olive diamonds and blue circles represent critical fields Hc1, Hc2 and Hc5, respectively, determined by τ(H). Error bars of Hc5 are transition widths in d(τ/H)/dH (Supplementary Fig. 2c). Purple diamonds are TN obtained from Cp(T) (Supplementary Fig. 7b; errors represent the half-widths of the peaks). Within the 1/3 magnetization plateau, a cascade of transitions appear in τ(H) as denoted by triangles in red (H∗ 1), magenta (H∗ 2) and yellow (the small step-like anomaly develops at low T, Fig. 2c).
